description_long: This is a medium-sized, slightly squashed cardboard parcel, sealed with multiple strips of brown and clear packing tape. The top surface features a shipping label with a barcode, QR code, and printed address information, partially covered by tape. A green and white decorative strip runs across the top and down the sides, possibly part of branded packaging or reused material. The cardboard is visibly creased, dented, and worn, suggesting the parcel has been handled extensively during transit. Some black printed symbols, such as arrows and handling marks, are visible on the sides. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics, typical of packages found on urban doorsteps or in delivery areas.
description: A worn, taped cardboard parcel with a shipping label and green decorative strip, showing signs of heavy handling and transit.
description_view_0: The front view shows the most dented side, with a large green patch and visible tape seams, as well as the edge of the shipping label.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the flatness of the parcel, the green and white strip running horizontally, and the shipping label positioned off-center.
description_view_2: The rear view displays more of the green strip and the taped seams, with the shipping label fully visible and some creasing on the cardboard.
description_view_3: This side view shows a relatively smooth surface with minor creases, the green strip, and black handling marks near the bottom edge.
